text: MARRIED. LENENTINE-DANFORTH. In Troy, March 31, Guy Lenentine of Harvey, Maine, and Miss Marguerite Danforth of Troy.

place: Monticello, Aroostook County, Maine (tried at Houlton)
text: MONTICELLO MAN LOSES LIBEL SUIT AGAINST TEACHER'S LANDLADY. (Special to the Commercial) Houlton, Nov. 24. A verdict for the defendants, Mr. and Mrs. Guy Lenentine of Monticello, was returned by the jury in the Supreme court at 9.30 o'clock last night in the case against them brought by Reginald Rush, also of Monticello, who alleged that Mr. and Mrs. Lenentine had libeled him and asked $10,000 damages. The case went to the jury at 5 o'clock after a trial that began Saturday and caused unusual interest throughout the county, bringing spectators from many towns especially Monticello, where the parties to the action belong. The plaintiff alleged that while he was courting a school teacher who was boarding at the Lenentines' home, the defendants wrote his mother a letter in which his character was defamed. The letter, it was claimed, was evidence for the defense, a warning to the parent that she should not keep company with the plaintiff [...] tention of the plaintiff in his suit, alleging damages. The defense was [based on the] truth of statements with [...] they testifying that [a] certain responsibility of [...] his boarding in their home. The trial was eagerly [followed by the] crowds that packed the [Court] House each day and [...] awaited with great [interest...] was a crowd in [court] when the jury returned [...]

place: Monticello / Mars Hill, Aroostook County, Maine
text: MONTICELLO. L. Leonard Temple, Limestone, spent the week-end at the home of Mr. and Mrs. G. C. Fletcher. Mr. and Mrs. Clifford Sharp of Stratham, N. H., visited Mrs. Elizabeth Sharp part of last week. Many from here attended the funeral services of Mrs. Edna, wife of Winfield Shaw at Mars Hill on Sunday. Mr. and Mrs. Almon Bell and Mrs. Althea Rackliffe of Mars Hill visited Mr. and Mrs. Guy Lenentine on Sunday. Mrs. Everett Buck and little daughter of Millinocket is visiting her mother, Mrs. Ward Harris. Mr. and Mrs. Delbert Carmichael of Fort Fairfield visited Mrs. Joseph Carmichael on Sunday. Mr. and Mrs. Benjamin Small have moved to Houlton. Mr. and Mrs. Ray McLaughlin spent Sunday at Princeton. Montford Hill of Grindstone is visiting his parents, Mr. and Mrs. P. O. Hill. Mrs. Burley Wootton entertained the Larkin Club at her home Tuesday afternoon. Miss Pauline Folsom, R. N., returned to Houlton last Saturday. Henry Bradbury returned home from Boston on Friday. Halston son of Mr. and Mrs. Guy Lenentine underwent an operation for tonsils last week at Mars Hill hospital. The operation was performed by Dr. Bundy. While at Mars Hill, Mrs. Lenentine was the guest of Mr. and Mrs. Hollis Bridges. A. M. Nason of Princeton spent Sunday with his family here. Mr. and Mrs. James Mitchell of Providence, R. I., are visiting relatives of this place. Mr. and Mrs. Robert Crandle of Claremont, N. H., were the guests of Mr. and Mrs. Benjamin F. Hanigan on Wednesday. J. D. Murphy of Bangor was a business caller here on Wednesday. Mrs. George Auber and Mrs. Walter Auber of Augusta, were guests of Mrs. Joel Wellington on Thursday. Mrs. Allison Smith of Stoulton was in town on Monday. Mrs. Donald Stackhouse is gaining after her recent sickness. Our teachers are attending the teachers convention at Houlton.

names_mentioned: Mr. and Mrs. O. B. Danforth
text: TROY. Their will be a Thanksgiving [dance] at the Grange hall Thursday night. Mr. and Mrs. Guy Lenentine and children of Montville have been spending a few days as guests of her parents, Mr. and Mrs. O. B. Danforth. Marguerite and Helen and Grace Lenentine were home Tuesday. Mrs. Lenentine and Haislon remaining for a few weeks. George Hilman, Henry Moore, Fernald and Homer Reynolds shot a nice deer the past week. Mr. and Mrs. H. B. Whitaker, [...] Linwood and Lee Small were [...] guests of her parents, Mr. and Mrs. Urial Reynolds. Mr. and Mrs. W. I. Coffin of [Fair]field was callers in town Sunday. F. M. Wingate and F. W. W[...] motored to Belfast, Sunday. O. R. Bagley has recently sold his farm to Fred Bean of Pittsfield. Mr. Bagley has bought the Waterhouse farm in Detroit village and will make his family there at once. W. R. Barnes spent the week[end at] home. Orman Gray had the good luck to shoot a deer recently. Dr. and Mrs. E. M. Soule have [gone] to Unity for the winter. Maurice Howes of Pittsfield [and] land waning of this town each [shot] a nice deer when they returned [from a] hunting trip. Guss Ward has recently moved his family to Unity where he has bought a farm. Several from this town attended church fair held at Unity last week. Porter Sanborn of Belfast was in town Friday. Mrs. Sadie Dodge spent Sun[day...]. Ira Parkhurst of Unity was [a busi]ness caller in town one day last [week]. Mrs. Blanche Richmond of P[ittsfield] visited her mother Mrs. Octave [...] more Friday. Superintendent of schools, G. [...] Grant of Unity was a business [caller] in town Friday. Schools in town closed Friday two weeks vacation. Mr. and Mrs. L. W. Knights [and] daughters, Dorothy and Betty, and Knights mother, Mrs. Marcia K[...] were calling on friends in town [Satur]day.

place: Belfast, Waldo County, Maine (died at Lynn, Mass.)
text: BELFAST, Nov. 26—Harry A. Bagley, formerly of Belfast, died at his home in Lynn on Sunday after a long illness. For several months he had been a great sufferer, and the end was expected. His aunt, Mrs. Ada Larrabee Wildes of Belfast was called to Lynn a few weeks ago and with his wife and two children were with him. Mr. Bagley had many friends in Belfast, where his boyhood was spent and where he married Miss Eva Cook, who survives him with their daughter and son. Funeral services were held in Lynn on Tuesday.
